Composite map + density figure

Source: R/green_journal.R
mysterycall_compose_map_density.Rd

Arranges a choropleth map above a marginal density/histogram plot in a 7:2 height ratio. Returns a grob suitable for ggplot2::ggsave() or save_green_journal_figure().

Usage

mysterycall_compose_map_density(
  map_plot,
  density_plot,
  map_weight = 7L,
  density_weight = 2L
)

Arguments

map_plot

A ggplot object. The choropleth / spatial map (upper panel).

density_plot

A ggplot object. The density or histogram (lower panel).

map_weight

Integer. Relative height for the map panel (default 7).

density_weight

Integer. Relative height for the density panel (default 2).

Value

A gridExtra grob (gtable). Pass to ggsave(plot = result) or save_green_journal_figure().

See also

save_green_journal_figure(), theme_green_journal_map()

Other green-journal-spatial: mysterycall_crs_albers_conus(), mysterycall_truncate_for_viz(), mysterycall_winsorize()

Examples

if (FALSE) { # interactive()
library(ggplot2)
p_map <- ggplot(tracts) + geom_sf(aes(fill = access)) +
  theme_green_journal_map()
p_den <- ggplot(tracts, aes(access)) +
  geom_density(fill = "#56B4E9", alpha = 0.6) +
  theme_green_journal()
composite <- mysterycall_compose_map_density(p_map, p_den)
save_green_journal_figure(composite, "figures/fig3")
}
